orientation: Practise bounds and error intervals using the same structure from the learn lesson.

worked_example.question: A mass is 3.2 kg correct to nearest 0.1 kg. Write the interval.

questions.question: A length is 7 cm to nearest cm. Give bounds.
A value is 2.4 to nearest 0.1. Give bounds.
A mass is 18.2 g to nearest 0.1 g. Find the upper bound.
A time is 52 s to nearest second. Find the lower bound.
A rectangle has length 8.4 cm and width 3.1 cm, both to nearest 0.1 cm. Find the upper bound for area.
questions.hint: Use upper bound for each dimension.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using the full rounding unit instead of half.
common_mistakes.correction: Bounds are half a rounding unit either side of the rounded value.
Upper bound is excluded, so use < on the upper inequality.

common_mistakes.mistake: Adding instead of subtracting for lower bound.
common_mistakes.correction: Lower bound is found by subtracting half the unit.

summary: Find half of the rounding unit.
Lower bound is inclusive and upper bound is exclusive.
Write intervals using correct inequality symbols.
